source_input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: Japandi neutral dining set with woven pendant
Minimal plant-forward living room with low wood table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.
pins.cluster_label: Japandi neutral dining set with woven pendant

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Minimal plant-forward living room with low wood table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi neutral dining set with woven pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round light-oak dining table, slim tapered legs, textured neutral upholstery, oversized woven pendant as focal point, ceramic tableware and hand-thrown vases. Flooring and paneling in pale oak unify the composition—stage with a single sculptural vase, linen place settings, and warm-toned tabletop ceramics.

cluster_summary.label: Low-floor Japanese reading/tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Intimate corner with floor cushions and low coffee tray, bamboo/shōji screening, layered natural-fiber rugs and woven poufs. Warm paper/lantern lighting creates deep amber glow—use soft cushions, round woven floor mats, low tray table and minimal potted greenery to recreate cozy scale.

cluster_summary.label: Minimal plant-forward living room with low wood table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Low, neutral sofa on wooden base, organic-shaped low coffee table, vertical wood slat accent and sparse framed art. Emphasize neutral textiles, textured rug, and multiple medium potted plants for vertical green accents—keep decor minimal: 2–3 dark ceramic vessels and one sculptural plant grouping.

rooms.name: Nest
rooms.windows: bay window / large glazed opening toward relax area

rooms.name: Dream
rooms.windows: single exterior window on top wall

rooms.name: Taste
rooms.windows: small window/vent above counter (adjacent exterior wall)

rooms.name: Dine
rooms.windows: bay-style multi-pane window on exterior corner

vibe_report.summary: Calm, minimal Japandi interiors tuned for a young family: warm neutrals, low-profile furniture, natural materials, and a compact cozy work corner. Prioritize layered textures (linen, jute, hand-thrown ceramics), sculptural plant groupings, and a single woven pendant as a dining focal point. Keep decor minimal and purposeful so sightlines between living, dining and kitchen feel cohesive and uncluttered.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: A warm-neutral palette built from Pale Oat (#EDE6DE) and Warm Sand (#D2BFA3) with Muted Clay (#B58E61) and Aged Oak (#8A6E52) accents. Use Moss Gray (#6A736A) and Charcoal (#2F3230) for grounding details. These tones keep the rooms feeling soft and warm while preserving material clarity in video passes.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient daylight from bay/exterior windows, supplemented by warm practicals: an oversized woven pendant over dining and warm paper/lantern task light in the reading nook. Derive all directional light from visible openings — windows and the pendant/lamp sources only. Shadows remain graphic but gentle for a cozy editorial look.
materials: light quarter-sawn oak (furniture & simple slatted accents)
flatwoven jute and low-pile natural wool rugs
furniture_language: simple tapered legs on tables and chairs
rounded table edges and organic coffee-table forms
open shelving with vertical spacing for objects
single sculptural plant groupings rather than many small pots
styling_rules: limit tabletop objects to 2–3 sculptural ceramics; avoid busy accessorizing
use one oversized woven pendant as the dining focal point; no competing ceiling fixtures in the same sightline
keep textiles tonal; introduce contrast with one Muted Clay accent per room (cushion or vessel)
place standing desk in living bay but keep it visually minimal — one slim task lamp and 2 curated objects
maintain vertical wood slat or framed art rhythm — max one small gallery per wall
avoid: heavy, dark upholstery that breaks the warm palette
high-backed, tall furniture that disrupts Japandi low silhouette
glossy ceramics or metallics that read as flashy
excessive clutter on shelves or tabletops

room_guidance.headline: Low-profile family living with a compact work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Place the living area around a low wooden coffee table and pale oat linen sofa facing the bay window; integrate a slim standing desk in the bay as the family's cozy work corner. Keep a single large potted plant near the bay to add vertical green without cluttering circulation between living, dining and kitchen.
must_include: low-profile 3-seater sofa — pale oat linen upholstery (#EDE6DE), dark walnut solid-wood recessed base, low back, single long seat cushion, approx 78in width
organic low coffee table — sculpted oval top in Muted Clay matte ceramic (#B58E61) with short Aged Oak legs (shared object)
standing desk — slim standing desk, Warm Sand laminate top (#D2BFA3) with matte black metal legs, minimal cable management (shared object)
must_avoid: tall armchairs or bulky high-back seating
bright colored textiles

room_guidance.headline: Serene, minimal bedroom with soft daylight
room_guidance.guidance: Use low bed platform in Aged Oak, pale linen bedding and two small hand-thrown ceramic bedside vases. Keep a single bookshelf for family storage and a small reading lamp. Maintain calm sightline into adjacent bathroom; avoid tall furniture near the doorway.
must_include: low platform bed in Aged Oak (#8A6E52) with pale oat linen bedding (#EDE6DE)
bookshelf — medium-height open bookshelf in Aged Oak, 4 shelves, slim vertical profile (user-provided planned object)
must_avoid: full-height wardrobes in the sightline to the bathroom
bright pattern bedding

room_guidance.headline: Compact, airy kitchen with warm wood touches
room_guidance.guidance: Keep counters minimal; use warm oak lower cabinets and matte black hardware. Small window above counter provides directional daylight. Use ceramic bowl or single vase on counter as staging. Avoid large island — circulation to dining must remain clear.
must_include: simple ceramic bowl or hand-thrown vase in Muted Clay on counter
visible window light as primary directional source
must_avoid: large kitchen island or oversized stools that obstruct flow to dining
bright stainless steel surfaces dominant in frame

room_guidance.headline: Intimate Japandi dining — round table and woven pendant
room_guidance.guidance: Center a round light-oak dining table with slim tapered legs beneath an oversized woven rattan pendant. Use 4 slim chairs in Warm Sand upholstery and a single sculptural vase on the table. Keep lamp/glow warm to differentiate from daylight while staying cohesive with living area tones.
must_include: round light-oak dining table — 42in diameter, light oak finish, slim tapered legs (shared object)
oversized woven pendant — rattan woven shade, warm amber inner diffuser, suspended low over table (shared object)
dining chairs — 4 slim dining chairs, Warm Sand upholstery (#D2BFA3), light oak tapered legs
must_avoid: large chandeliers or multiple competing ceiling fixtures
too-large table ( >48in) that crowds the 7.8ft x 8ft room

room_guidance.headline: Small, calm bathroom with frosted window light
room_guidance.guidance: Use a simple floating vanity in Aged Oak with matte black hardware, a single round mirror, and a small ceramic soap dispenser. Diffuse light from the frosted window and a warm sconce if needed. Keep visual connection to bedroom tidy—no laundry piles in frame.
must_include: floating vanity in Aged Oak with matte black hardware
small hand-thrown ceramic soap dispenser in Muted Clay
must_avoid: bright clinical whites or high-gloss chrome fixtures
cluttered counters

warnings: Kitchen and bathroom dimensions were not provided by ML; verify clearances before final staging and camera moves.
Standing desk in living bay must not block circulation between nest and dine—keep to slim profile and place flush to window.

creative_spec.overall_mood: calm minimal warmth with organic, textural layering
global_style_notes: Preserve architecture and exact layout; verticals must remain true in all image passes and video.
Derive all directional lighting from visible windows and practical fixtures only—no introduced fill lights from non-visible sources.
Maintain consistent color temperature across all rooms (warm daylight, ~3200–4000K practicals).
Use low cinematic contrast with preserved midtone detail for family-friendly readability.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift, no color inconsistency between rooms, no mismatched furniture across sightlines

caption_context.property_brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.
caption_context.aesthetic_summary: Calm, minimal Japandi interiors tuned for a young family: warm neutrals, low-profile furniture, natural materials, and a compact cozy work corner. Prioritize layered textures (linen, jute, hand-thrown ceramics), sculptural plant groupings, and a single woven pendant as a dining focal point. Keep decor minimal and purposeful so sightlines between living, dining and kitchen feel cohesive and uncluttered.
